Identity.Name: StingInsulationTypes
Identity.Definition: Insulation type codes per BS 5422 — selects the K-value, surface emissivity, fire performance, and embodied-carbon defaults for pipe/duct insulation. Drives thickness sizing in IceCarbonCalculator and pipe-loss models, plus fire-rating verification on penetration seals.

Governance.StandardsBasis: BS 5422:2009; BS EN ISO 13787; ICE Database v3.0 (embodied carbon)

Value.Definition: Phenolic Foam — closed-cell, k=0.020 W/m·K. Low fire risk, BS EN 13501 Class A2. STING default for cold pipework.
Mineral (Rock) Wool — k=0.035 W/m·K. Non-combustible A1. Standard for hot pipework, ducting acoustic lining.
Glass Wool — k=0.034 W/m·K. Non-combustible A1. Duct lining + thermal lagging.
Polyurethane (PUR) — k=0.022 W/m·K. Used for pre-insulated pipework. Reaction-to-fire grade varies.
Polyisocyanurate (PIR) — k=0.021 W/m·K. Improved fire performance over PUR.
Closed-cell Elastomeric (Armaflex / equivalent) — k=0.033 W/m·K. Anti-condensation, flexible.
Calcium Silicate — k=0.06 W/m·K. High-temperature steam / HTHW; non-combustible A1.
Aerogel Blanket — k=0.014 W/m·K. Ultra-low-conductivity, used where space-constrained.
Vacuum Insulated Panel (VIP) — k=0.004 W/m·K. Specialist; cold-store + ultra-low-energy details.
No insulation — bare-pipe or exposed-duct sections. Carry for completeness in BOQ.
